revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Japannese Argipel]][[ar:الأرخبيل الياباني]][[ast:Archipiélagu xaponés]][[az:Yapon arxipelaqı]][[be:Японскія астравы]][[bg:Японски острови]][[bn:জাপান দ্বীপপুঞ্জ]][[bs:Japanski arhipelag]][[ca:Arxipèlag japonès]][[cs:Japonské souostroví]][[da:Japanske øhav]][[de:Japanischer Archipel]][[el:Αρχιπέλαγος της Ιαπωνίας]][[en:Japanese archipelago]][[eo:Japana insularo]][[es:Archipiélago japonés]][[et:Jaapani saarestik]][[eu:Japoniar uhartedia]][[fa:مجمع‌الجزایر ژاپن]][[fr:Archipel japonais]][[gl:Arquipélago xaponés]][[hi:जापानी द्वीपसमूह]][[hr:Japansko otočje]][[hu:Japán-szigetek]][[hy:Ճապոնական կղզիներ]][[ia:Archipelago japonese]][[id:Kepulauan Jepang]][[ja:日本列島]][[ka:იაპონიის არქიპელაგი]][[ko:일본 열도]][[ku:Komgirava Japonî]][[lt:Japonijos salynas]][[lv:Japānas salu arhipelāgs]][[min:Kapulauan Japang]][[mk:Јапонски Архипелаг]][[ms:Kepulauan Jepun]][[nl:Japanse archipel]][[pl:Wyspy Japońskie]][[pnb:جاپانی مجموعہ الجزائر]][[pt:Arquipélago japonês]][[ro:Arhipelagul japonez]][[ru:Японские острова]][[sd:جاپاني ٻيٽن جو ميڙ]][[sh:Japanski arhipelag]][[si:ජපන් දූපත් සමූහය]][[sk:Japonské ostrovy]][[sl:Japonsko otočje]][[sq:Arkipelagu japonez]][[th:หมู่เกาะญี่ปุ่น]][[tl:Kapuluang Hapones]][[tr:Japon Takımadaları]][[uk:Японський архіпелаг]][[ur:جاپانی مجمع الجزائر]][[uz:Yapon arxipelagi]][[vi:Quần đảo Nhật Bản]][[wuu:日本列岛]][[zh-min-nan:Ji̍t-pún lia̍t-tó]][[zh-yue:日本列島]][[zh:日本列島]]
{{Short description|Archipelago off the coast of Northeast Asia}} {{Infobox islands | name = Japanese Archipelago | image_name = Satellite View of Japan 1999.jpg | image_caption = A satellite image of the main archipelago (Ryukyu Islands and South Kuril Islands not pictured) | coordinates = {{Coord|36|N|138|E|type:isle_region:JP_scale:10000000|display=inline,title}} | country = Japan | area_km2 = 377,975 }}
The '''Japanese archipelago''' ({{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|qid=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}}: {{Lang|ja|日本列島}}, ''Nihon Rettō'') is an {{ill|Archipelago|en|Archipelago|qid=Q33837|lt=Archipelago|1=Archipelago}} of {{ill|en:List of islands of Japan|en|en:List of islands of Japan|qid=Q1125331|lt=14,125 islands|1=List of islands of Japan}} that form the country of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref name="recount">{{cite web |title=Recount with digital map leads to doubling of listed Japanese islands |website=Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/02/15/national/japanese-islands-recount/ |date=Feb 15, 2023 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230215042328/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/02/15/national/japanese-islands-recount/ |archive-date=Feb 15, 2023}}</ref> It extends over {{cvt|3000|km|mi}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Water Supply in Japan |format=website |url=https://www.mhlw.go.jp/english/policy/health/water_supply/1.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180126130519/https://www.mhlw.go.jp/english/policy/health/water_supply/1.html |archive-date=January 26, 2018 |publisher=Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare |access-date=2018-09-26}}</ref> from the {{ill|Sea of Okhotsk|en|Sea of Okhotsk|qid=Q41602|lt=Sea of Okhotsk|1=Sea of Okhotsk}} in the northeast to the {{ill|East China Sea|en|East China Sea|qid=Q45341|lt=East China|1=East China Sea}} and {{ill|Philippine Sea|en|Philippine Sea|qid=Q159183|lt=Philippine|1=Philippine Sea}} seas in the southwest along the {{ill|Pacific coast|en|Pacific coast|qid=Q430265|lt=Pacific coast|1=Pacific coast}} of the {{ill|Eurasia|en|Eurasia|qid=Q5401|lt=Eurasia|1=Eurasia}}n continent, and consists of three {{ill|Island arc|en|Island arc|qid=Q1515058|lt=Island arc|1=Island arc}}s from north to south: the {{ill|Northeastern Japan Arc|en|Northeastern Japan Arc|qid=Q11526209|lt=Northeastern Japan Arc|1=Northeastern Japan Arc}}, the Southwestern Japan Arc, and the {{ill|Ryukyu Islands|en|Ryukyu Islands|qid=Q6897416|lt=Ryukyu Island Arc|1=Ryukyu Islands}}. The {{ill|Daitō Islands|en|Daitō Islands|qid=Q697792|lt=Daitō Islands|1=Daitō Islands}}, the [[:en:Izu–Bonin–Mariana Arc|Izu–Bonin–Mariana Arc]], the {{ill|Kuril Islands|en|Kuril Islands|qid=Q80389|lt=Kuril Islands|1=Kuril Islands}}, and the {{ill|Nanpō Islands|en|Nanpō Islands|qid=Q1964385|lt=Nanpō Islands|1=Nanpō Islands}} neighbor the {{ill|Archipelago|en|Archipelago|qid=Q33837|lt=Archipelago|1=Archipelago}}.
Japan is the largest {{ill|island country|en|island country|qid=Q112099|lt=island country|1=Island country}} in {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|qid=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}} and the {{ill|list of island countries|en|list of island countries|qid=Q6859151|lt=fourth-largest island country|1=List of island countries}} in the world with {{convert|377,975.24|km²|sqmi|abbr=on}}.<ref name="world-atlas">{{cite web |url=https://www.worldatlas.com/articles/which-are-the-island-countries-of-the-world.html |title=Island Countries Of The World |publisher=WorldAtlas.com |access-date=2019-08-10 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171207094959/http://www.worldatlas.com/articles/which-are-the-island-countries-of-the-world.html | archive-date=2017-12-07}}</ref><ref name="Geospatial Information Authority of Japan 2019">{{cite web |title=令和元年全国都道府県市区町村別面積調（１０月１日時点）, Reiwa 1st year National area of each prefecture municipality (as of October 1) |url=https://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO201910-index.html |publisher=[[Geospatial Information Authority of Japan]] |access-date=2 January 2020 |language=ja |date=26 December 2019 |archive-date=15 April 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200415123703/https://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO201910-index.html |url-status=live }}</ref> It has an {{ill|Exclusive economic zone of Japan|en|Exclusive economic zone of Japan|qid=Q11505956|lt=exclusive economic zone|1=Exclusive economic zone of Japan}} of {{convert|4,470,000|km2|mi2|abbr=on}}.<ref name="kaiho">{{cite web |url=http://www1.kaiho.mlit.go.jp/JODC/ryokai/ryokai_setsuzoku.html |title=日本の領海等概念図 |publisher=海上保安庁海洋情報部| access-date=12 August 2018| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180812151410/http://www1.kaiho.mlit.go.jp/JODC/ryokai/ryokai_setsuzoku.html| archive-date=August 12, 2018}}</ref>
==Terminology== The term "{{ill|Mainland Japan|en|Mainland Japan|qid=Q4113790|lt=Mainland Japan|1=Mainland Japan}}" is used to distinguish the large islands of the Japanese archipelago from the remote, smaller islands; it refers to the main islands of [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[Honshu]], [[Kyushu]], and [[Shikoku]].<ref name="main-islands-japan">{{cite web |script-title=ja:離島とは(島の基礎知識) |trans-title=what is a remote island? |publisher=[[Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism]] |url=http://www.mlit.go.jp/crd/chirit/ritoutoha.html |website=MLIT (Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism) |date=22 August 2015 |access-date= 9 August 2019 |language=ja |format=website |quote=MILT classification 6,852 islands(main islands: 5 islands, remote islands: 6,847 islands) |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071113053915/http://www.mlit.go.jp/crd/chirit/ritoutoha.html |archive-date=2007-11-13}}</ref> From 1943 until the end of the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, {{ill|Karafuto Prefecture|en|Karafuto Prefecture|qid=Q696736|lt=Karafuto Prefecture|1=Karafuto Prefecture}} (south {{ill|Sakhalin|en|Sakhalin|qid=Q7792|lt=Sakhalin|1=Sakhalin}}) was designated part of the mainland. Geographically speaking the term "mainland" is somewhat inaccurate, as this refers to an expanse of territory that is attached to a continental landmass.
The term "home islands" was used at the end of World War II to define the area where Japanese sovereignty and constitutional rule of its {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} would be restricted. The term is also commonly used today to distinguish the archipelago from Japan's colonies and other territories.<ref>Milton W. Meyer, ''Japan: A Concise History'', fourth ed. Lanham, Maryland: Rowman & Littlefield, 2012, {{ISBN|9780742541184}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=d11lJ2JKN4wC&dq=Japan+archipelago+Home+Islands&pg=PA2 p. 2] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230226040610/https://books.google.com/books?id=d11lJ2JKN4wC&pg=PA2&dq=Japan+archipelago+Home+Islands&hl=en&sa=X&ei=Wa_IUZGgFKSviALPqoG4DA&ved=0CDkQ6AEwAQ#v=onepage&q=Japan%20archipelago%20Home%20Islands&f=false |date=2023-02-26 }}.</ref>
== Palaeogeography == {{main|Geology of Japan}} {{Gallery |title=Changes of land-forms of Japan over time |width=160 | height=170 |align=center |footer= |File:Sea of Japan Early Miocene map.svg |Japanese archipelago, Sea of Japan and surrounding part of continental {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|qid=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}} in {{ill|Early Miocene|en|Early Miocene|qid=Q5326689|lt=Early Miocene|1=Early Miocene}} (23–18 Ma) |File:Sea of Japan Pliocene map.svg |Japanese archipelago, Sea of Japan and surrounding part of continental {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|qid=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}} in [[:en:Middle Pliocene|Middle Pliocene]] to [[:en:Late Pliocene|Late Pliocene]] (3.5–2 Ma) |File:Japan glaciation.gif |Japanese archipelago at the {{ill|Last Glacial Maximum|en|Last Glacial Maximum|qid=Q1979625|lt=Last Glacial Maximum|1=Last Glacial Maximum}} about 20,000 years ago, thin black line indicates present-day shorelines: {{legend|#fbad5d|Vegetated land}} {{legend|white|Unvegetated land}} {{legend|#acfefc|Ocean}} }}
{{Clear}}
==Geography== {{Main|Geography of Japan}} {{See also|Prefectures of Japan}}
The archipelago consists of 14,125 islands<ref name="recount">{{cite web |title=Recount with digital map leads to doubling of listed Japanese islands |website=Japan Times |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/02/15/national/japanese-islands-recount/ |date=Feb 15, 2023 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230215042328/https://www.japantimes.co.jp/news/2023/02/15/national/japanese-islands-recount/ |archive-date=Feb 15, 2023}}</ref> (here defined as land more than 100 m in circumference), of which 430 are inhabited.<ref>{{cite magazine |title=Islands in Abundance |magazine=Look Japan |year=1997 |volume=43 |issue=493–504 |publisher=Limited |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=NHi6AAAAIAAJ&q=%226%2C852+islands%22 |page=35 |access-date=2015-10-18 |archive-date=2023-02-26 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230226040609/https://books.google.com/books?id=NHi6AAAAIAAJ&q=%226%2C852+islands%22 |url-status=live }}</ref> The five main islands, from north to south, are [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]], [[Honshu]], [[Shikoku]], [[Kyushu]], and {{ill|Okinawa Island|en|Okinawa Island|qid=Q600614|lt=Okinawa|1=Okinawa Island}}.<ref name="main-islands-japan" /> Honshu is the largest and referred to as the {{ill|Mainland Japan|en|Mainland Japan|qid=Q4113790|lt=Japanese mainland|1=Mainland Japan}}.<ref>[http://www.thefreedictionary.com/Japanese+Archipelago "Japanese Archipelago"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180919195043/https://www.thefreedictionary.com/Japanese+Archipelago |date=2018-09-19 }}, [[:en:TheFreeDictionary.com|TheFreeDictionary.com]], retrieved 24 June 2013.</ref>
The topography is divided as: *Hokkaido, Honshu, and Shikoku and its surrounding islands; *Kyushu and the [[:en:Ryukyu arc|Ryukyu arc]], which is composed of the Ryukyu Islands and other surrounding islands; *Eastern part of Hokkaido and the {{ill|Kuril Islands|en|Kuril Islands|qid=Q80389|lt=Kuril Islands|1=Kuril Islands}}; *{{ill|Nanpō Islands|en|Nanpō Islands|qid=Q1964385|lt=Nanpō Islands|1=Nanpō Islands}} and the {{ill|Izu Peninsula|en|Izu Peninsula|qid=Q822822|lt=Izu Peninsula|1=Izu Peninsula}} (part of [[:en:Izu–Bonin–Mariana Arc|Izu-Bonin-Mariana Arc]]).
===Image gallery=== {{Gallery |title= |width=160 | height=170 |align=center |footer= |File:Ogasawara islands.png |The {{ill|Nanpō Islands|en|Nanpō Islands|qid=Q1964385|lt=Nanpō Islands|1=Nanpō Islands}} stretch to the southeast and are administered by the [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo Metropolis]].|File:Location_of_the_Ryukyu_Islands.JPG |The {{ill|Ryukyu Islands|en|Ryukyu Islands|qid=Q6897416|lt=Ryukyu Islands|1=Ryukyu Islands}}, which stretch towards {{ill|Taiwan|en|Taiwan|qid=Q865|lt=Taiwan|1=Taiwan}}, are administered by {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} and {{ill|Okinawa Prefecture|en|Okinawa Prefecture|qid=Q766445|lt=Okinawa Prefecture|1=Okinawa Prefecture}}.|File:Japan_Relief_Map_of_Land_and_Seabed.png |Seabed relief map, showing surface and underwater terrain and islands such as Minami-Tori-Shima, Benten-jima, Okinotorishima, and Yonaguni}}
==See also== *[[Sea of Japan#Geography and geology|Japan in the Paleolithic]] *{{ill|List of extreme points of Japan|en|List of extreme points of Japan|qid=Q1059347|lt=List of extreme points of Japan|1=List of extreme points of Japan}} *{{ill|en:List of islands of Japan|en|en:List of islands of Japan|qid=Q1125331|lt=en:List of islands of Japan|1=List of islands of Japan}}
